q: Transition elements such as iron and copper have played an important role in our lives.
The first transition element of 2d series is 21 to 30.
Iron used as a catalyst in the manufacture of sulphur trioxide.
Iron is present in haemoglobin.
Cupronickel alloys are poorly resistance to corrosion by seawater.
Cupronickel alloys are used in shipbuilding.
Copper is a major component in modern jet aircraft and is used in manufacturing artificial joints and dental implants.
Transition elements are located between Groups 2 and 12 elements of the periodic table.
The d-block elements of the Periodic Table are transition elements.
Transition elements are located between s block and p block elements of the periodic table.
IUPAC defines transition elements are elements that have a completely filled d sub shell only.
IUPAC defines transition elements are elements that have an incompletely filled d orbital only.
The d-block, elements start with 4 period.
Lanthanide and actinide series are called the outer transition elements.
The inner transition elements are also called the p-block elements.
The outer transition elements are also called the d-block elements.
The second series 3d transition elements run from yttrium (39Y) through cadmium (48Cd).
The lanthanide series from lanthanum (57La) to mercury (80Hg).
According to IUPAC, Zn is not transition elements because it has completely filled 3d sub shell.
The first row of the transition elements is in the fourth row (Period 4) of the Periodic Table.
Atomic radii of d-block elements in a series increases with increase in atomic number.
The trend of IE correlates with an increase in the nuclear charge and a decrease in the atomic radius.
In the 3d series, there are 10 transition elements.
In the 3d series, atomic number increases from left to right so zinc has the smallest atomic size.
The 3d orbitals is slightly lower in energy than the 4s orbitals.
The 4s orbital is slightly higher in energy than the 3d orbitals.
In atoms of the transition elements, the 4s orbital is normally filled first.
The filling of the 3d orbital in 30Zn is incompletely filled.
The filling of the 3d orbitals generally obeys Hund's rule.
The 3d series of transition elements have an inner electron configuration of [Kr].
24Cu can also be represented using the argon core as [Ar] 4s² 3d⁴.
In the 3d transition metal elements, the 4s orbital is the outermost orbital.
The ions of 3d transition elements have an essential electronic configuration of (n-1)d¹⁻¹⁰ ns¹⁻².
The zinc element has completely filled 3d orbitals.
Completely filled 3d orbitals are so stable that zinc can form compounds in the +3 oxidation state.
Copper is considered a transition element because the Cu has incomplete 3d orbitals.
Many of the properties of transition elements are similar as the same as other Group I and II metals.
Cu²⁺ ion has the electronic configuration, [Ar] 3d⁹.
Transition elements exhibit fixed valency because they release electrons from (n-1)d orbitals.
The electrons are removed from the 4s orbital before 3d orbital.
Most of 3 d transition elements have silvery-white in colour.
The colour of light absorbed by an aqueous solution of Cu²⁺ is yellow.
The transition elements have a low density.
All the 3 d transition elements have silvery-white in colour.
Mass of a 1 cm³ of iron is 8 times heavier than that of a 1 cm³ cube of sodium.
Transition elements are generally poor conductors of electricity and heat.
Transition elements have high melting points and boiling points, and a high density.
Transition elements are significantly less reactive than Group I metals.
In general, platinum metal does not corrode readily in the atmosphere.
Transition elements exhibit fixed oxidation states.
The transition elements show multiple oxidation states.
The +3 oxidation states are more stable at the end of the 3 d transition series.
The stable oxidation state of Ti is + 4.
The elements in the middle of the series exhibit more oxidation states than those to the left or right.
The highest oxidation state of manganese (25Mn) is + 6.
The highest oxidation state of iron (26Fe) is + 6.
In the 3d series, the highest oxidation states of transition elements are reducing agents.
Sc³⁺ ion is colourless because Sc³⁺ ion has completely filled d orbitals.
Zn²⁺ ion is colourless because Zn²⁺ ion has completely empty d orbitals.
The colour of compounds of transition metals is due to the presence of unpaired electrons.
The transition metals readily form alloys.
If the electrons are unpaired, then it is a diamagnetic substance.
All transition elements and their compounds are paramagnetic.
Copper (29Cu) considered a paramagnetic substance.
The transition elements are very useful as catalysts.
Many transition elements and their compounds act as good catalysts for all reactions.
Iron is used as a catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia (Haber process).
Vanadium is used in the form of vanadium(V) oxide (V₂O₅) in the manufacture of sulphuric acid (Contact process).
Nickel is used as a catalyst in the hydrogenation of alkenes.
Copper is used for electrical wire, circuit boards, water pipes, and industrial machinery.
